---
name: token-system
description: >
  Rules for the OUDS 3-tier design token system. Covers which tokens to use where,
  auto-generated file protections, CSS custom property usage for dark mode,
  and the color-mode mixin. Applied when working on token-related files or SCSS
  that consumes tokens.
applyTo: "**/packages/**/*.scss,scss/_variables*.scss,scss/_root.scss"
---

# Token System Rules

## Why tokens matter

The token system is the mechanism that makes one codebase serve 3 brands. Component SCSS references token variables; each brand provides different values for those variables. Breaking the token contract breaks multi-brand support.

---

## Token hierarchy (quick reference)

```
Raw ($core-ouds-*, $core-orange-*)  →  never use in components
  ↓
Semantic ($ouds-space-*, $ouds-border-radius-*)  →  use for non-color values
  ↓
Component ($ouds-button-*, $ouds-chip-*)  →  preferred when available
  ↓
CSS custom props (--bs-color-*)  →  use for all colors (enables dark mode)
```

For the full architecture and pipeline details, read `.github/instructions/references/token-architecture.md`.

---

## Rules

### 1. Use component tokens first, then semantic tokens

Check if `$ouds-<component>-<property>-<variant>` exists before falling back to `$ouds-<category>-<variant>`.

```scss
// ✓ component token exists
@include border-radius($ouds-button-border-radius-default);

// ✓ no component token — semantic fallback
@include border-radius($ouds-border-radius-default);
```

### 2. Use CSS custom properties for all colors

Color tokens come in light/dark pairs. They're exposed as CSS custom properties to enable runtime theme switching. Always consume via `var()`:

```scss
// ✓ — dark mode works automatically
color: var(--#{$prefix}color-content-default);
background: var(--#{$prefix}color-bg-primary);

// ✗ — locked to one mode
color: $ouds-color-content-default-light;
```

### 3. Never use raw tokens

Raw tokens (`$core-ouds-*`, `$core-orange-*`, `$core-sosh-*`) are primitives with no semantic meaning. Using them couples your code to the primitive layer and breaks when token values are redesigned.

### 4. Never edit auto-generated files

These 5 files per brand are generated by the Figma → DTCG → Style Dictionary pipeline:

- `_raw.scss`
- `_semantic.scss`
- `_component.scss`
- `_semantic-colors-custom-props.scss`
- `_component-colors-custom-props.scss`

Only `_composite.scss` is hand-editable (for elevation, font-stacks, SVG icons, Sass maps).

### 5. Respect import order

In `packages/<brand>/scss/tokens/_index.scss`:

```
1. _raw.scss                          (primitives)
2. _semantic.scss                     (aliases)
3. _semantic-colors-custom-props.scss (CSS props + SCSS redefinitions)
4. _composite.scss                    (elevation/fonts/icons)
5. _component-colors-custom-props.scss
6. _component.scss                    (component tokens)
```

`_composite.scss` must come after `_semantic-colors-custom-props.scss` because it uses the redefined color variables (which now point to `var()`).

### 6. New tokens → all 3 brands

When introducing a new token variable, it must be added to all 3 brand packages. Each brand can define a different value.

If the design pipeline hasn't delivered the token yet, add a temporary fallback in `scss/_variables.scss` with a `// TODO:` comment:

```scss
// TODO: replace with OUDS token when pipeline delivers
$ouds-my-component-border-radius: $ouds-border-radius-default !default;
```

### 7. Use `color-mode()` mixin for theme declarations

```scss
@include color-mode(light, true) {
  --#{$prefix}color-action-enabled: #{$ouds-color-action-enabled-light};
}
@include color-mode(dark) {
  --#{$prefix}color-action-enabled: #{$ouds-color-action-enabled-dark};
}
```

---

## Token naming convention

| Layer | Pattern | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Raw | `$core-ouds-{category}-{scale}` | `$core-ouds-border-radius-200` |
| Raw (brand) | `$core-{brand}-{category}-{name}` | `$core-orange-color-orange-550` |
| Semantic | `$ouds-{category}-{variant}` | `$ouds-border-radius-default` |
| Semantic color | `$ouds-color-{name}-{light\|dark}` | `$ouds-color-action-enabled-light` |
| Composite | `$ouds-{name}` | `$ouds-elevation-raised` |
| Component | `$ouds-{component}-{category}-{variant}` | `$ouds-button-border-radius-default` |
| CSS prop | `--{$prefix}{category}-{name}` | `--bs-color-action-enabled` |
